patch:
diff --git a/.gitignore b/.gitignore
index 551b41b..5061ec4 100644
--- a/.gitignore
+++ b/.gitignore
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 # Pyenv
 .python-version
+.ipynb_checkpoints/
 
 # Byte-compiled / optimized / DLL files
 __pycache__/
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 6a18564..7de3b72 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -28,7 +28,7 @@ lint_diff format_diff: PYTHON_FILES=$(shell git diff --relative=. --name-only --
 lint lint_diff:
 	[ "$(PYTHON_FILES)" = "" ] ||	uv run ruff format $(PYTHON_FILES) --diff
 	[ "$(PYTHON_FILES)" = "" ] ||	uv run ruff check $(PYTHON_FILES) --diff
-	[ "$(PYTHON_FILES)" = "" ] || uv run mypy $(PYTHON_FILES)
+	[ "$(PYTHON_FILES)" = "" ] || uvx ty check $(PYTHON_FILES)
 
 format format_diff:
 	[ "$(PYTHON_FILES)" = "" ] || uv run ruff check --fix $(PYTHON_FILES)
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index c1943cf..ceba27c 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -229,6 +229,7 @@ from langchain_core.tools import tool, BaseTool, InjectedToolCallId
 from langchain_core.messages import ToolMessage
 from langgraph.types import Command
 from langgraph.prebuilt import InjectedState
+from langgraph_supervisor.handoff import METADATA_KEY_HANDOFF_DESTINATION
 
 def create_custom_handoff_tool(*, agent_name: str, name: str | None, description: str | None) -> BaseTool:
 
@@ -261,6 +262,7 @@ def create_custom_handoff_tool(*, agent_name: str, name: str | None, description
             },
         )
 
+    handoff_to_agent.metadata = {METADATA_KEY_HANDOFF_DESTINATION: agent_name}
     return handoff_to_agent
 ```
 
diff --git a/langgraph_supervisor/supervisor.py b/langgraph_supervisor/supervisor.py
index b511f02..ff10eb2 100644
--- a/langgraph_supervisor/supervisor.py
+++ b/langgraph_supervisor/supervisor.py
@@ -57,7 +57,7 @@ def _supports_disable_parallel_tool_calls(model: LanguageModelLike) -> bool:
 
 
 def _make_call_agent(
-    agent: Pregel,
+    agent: Pregel[Any],
     output_mode: OutputMode,
     add_handoff_back_messages: bool,
     supervisor_name: str,
@@ -181,7 +181,7 @@ def _prepare_tool_node(
         if input_tool_node is not None:
             tool_node = ToolNode(
                 all_tools,
-                name=input_tool_node.name,
+                name=str(input_tool_node.name),
                 tags=list(input_tool_node.tags) if input_tool_node.tags else None,
                 handle_tool_errors=input_tool_node.handle_tool_errors,
                 messages_key=input_tool_node.messages_key,
diff --git a/pyproject.toml b/pyproject.toml
index bbf90c0..e4ab69d 100644
--- a/pyproject.toml
+++ b/pyproject.toml
@@ -60,3 +60,10 @@ warn_return_any = true
 warn_unused_configs = true
 disallow_untyped_defs = true
 check_untyped_defs = true
+
+[tool.ty.rules]
+no-matching-overload = "ignore" 
+call-non-callable = "ignore"
+unresolved-import = "ignore"
+[tool.ty.src]
+exclude = ["tests"]
\ No newline at end of file

problem_statement:
`create_supervisor` generates and adds handoff tools ( `transfer_to_{agent_name}` ) even when custom ones are given
Hi! Thank you for implementing such a useful utility. Here I'd like to report a bug related to the automatic creation of handoff tools.

# What's happening

Even when custom handoff tools like the one in the [README](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph-supervisor-py?tab=readme-ov-file#customizing-handoff-tools) following `Here is an example of what a custom handoff tool might look like:` are implemented, default handoff tools named `transfer_to_{agent_name}` are generated and added to the agent. This results in either of the following situations: 1. Two handoff tools with different names transferring to the same agent are bound to the supervisor, 2. The definitions of automatically created handoff tools overwrite custom ones.

# Why it's happening

It seems this is due to the behavior of `_get_handoff_destinations` function that is used in `_prepare_tool_node` function inside `create_supervisor`. Following is what's happening behind the scene:

1. I pass custom handoff tools to `create_supervisor()`.
2. Inside `create_supervisor()`, `_prepare_tool_node()` is called. 
3. Inside `_prepare_tool_node()`, my custom handoff tools (stored in the `tools` argument) are passed to `_get_handoff_destinations()`
4. **Here, each tool is checked if it has `metadata` property and `METADATA_KEY_HANDOFF_DESTINATION` key in it**
    https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph-supervisor-py/blob/9dacf15857a356806a764ac6e3884bbb3cead9ee/langgraph_supervisor/supervisor.py#L120-L133
5. Since my custom handoff tools don't have a `metadata` property, none of my tools are regarded as valid handoff tools. Thus the if block below will navigate to the else branch and handoff tools are created
    https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph-supervisor-py/blob/9dacf15857a356806a764ac6e3884bbb3cead9ee/langgraph_supervisor/supervisor.py#L154-L190

# Mitigation

We can imitate the implementation of `create_handoff_tool` 

https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph-supervisor-py/blob/9dacf15857a356806a764ac6e3884bbb3cead9ee/langgraph_supervisor/handoff.py#L122-L125

by manually adding `metadata` property to our custom handoff tool. This prevents the duplication of handoff tools.

```python
from typing import Annotated

from langchain_core.tools import tool, BaseTool, InjectedToolCallId
from langchain_core.messages import ToolMessage
from langgraph.types import Command
from langgraph.prebuilt import InjectedState

+ from langgraph_supervisor.handoff import METADATA_KEY_HANDOFF_DESTINATION

def create_custom_handoff_tool(*, agent_name: str, name: str | None, description: str | None) -> BaseTool:

    @tool(name, description=description)
    def handoff_to_agent(
        # you can add additional tool call arguments for the LLM to populate
        # for example, you can ask the LLM to populate a task description for the next agent
        task_description: Annotated[str, "Detailed description of what the next agent should do, including all of the relevant context."],
        # you can inject the state of the agent that is calling the tool
        state: Annotated[dict, InjectedState],
        tool_call_id: Annotated[str, InjectedToolCallId],
    ):
        ...

+   handoff_to_agent.metadata = {METADATA_KEY_HANDOFF_DESTINATION: agent_name}

    return handoff_to_agent
```

# Suggestion

I think the example in README or the validation logic in `_get_handoff_destinations()` should be modified. Thank you for reading my lengthy bug report 🙏
